commands.review_summary:
The PR lets Discord send the configured initial ack reaction for `room_event` messages when `messages.ackReactionScope` is `"all"` and adds focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +36. Total +37 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current `main` and v2026.5.26 suppress Discord room-event ack reactions before the shared `"all"` scope can apply. I did not run tests because this review is read-only, but the current source and linked issue reproduce the path clearly.
commands.review_followup: Manual review is needed because the protected maintainer label, missing live proof, and cross-channel room-event policy decision are not a narrow automated repair task.

target.title: [codex] Honor Discord all ack scope for room events
target.body:
## Summary
- lets Discord send the configured initial ack reaction for `room_event` messages when `messages.ackReactionScope` is `"all"`
- keeps lifecycle status reactions suppressed for room events unless that behavior is changed separately
- adds regression coverage for both forced-all and non-forcing room-event scopes

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#87368.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts -t 'room events'`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts`

Behavior addressed: Discord room-event messages with `ackReactionScope: "all"` now receive the configured initial ack reaction.
Real environment tested: local macOS source worktree at `upstream/main`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused Vitest command above.
Evidence after fix: 3 matching room-event tests passed, including the new `"all"` ack case.
Observed result after fix: non-forcing scopes still suppress room-event reactions; `"all"` sends the configured ack.
What was not tested: live Discord guild/thread delivery; this PR validates the message-handler decision path with existing Discord monitor test harnesses.
target.author: scoootscooob
